The Court makes the following ruling:

Status Conference Re: Progress Of Petition For Assumption Of Jurisdiction Over Respondent'S Unauthorized Law Practice. Judge Schulman's order entred on April 29, 2020 sunsets on April 29, 2022. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jun-10-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Kerri C. Riley (213-765-1236; Kerri.Riley@calbar.ca.gov), Deputy Trial Counsel from Office of Chief Trial Counsel for Petitioner, State Bar of California.
Nelly Allen Gabueva (415-534-5473; allenelly@gmail.com) Respondent in propria persona.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT NELLY ALLEN's Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Jurisdiction:

Respondent Nelly Allen Gabueva's "motion to dismiss for lack of jurisdiction" is denied. The Court had (and retains) jurisdiction over Respondent under Article 7, Chapter 4 of the Business & Professions Code, Unlawful Practice of Law. By order filed April 29, 2020, this Court granted the State Bar's application for a permanent order assuming jurisdiction over Respondent's unauthorized law practice. In that order, the Court found among other things that although Respondent has never been an active member of the California State Bar and has never been otherwise authorized pursuant to statute or court rule to practice law in California, she nevertheless was advertising or holding herself out as practicing or entitled to practice law in California. Respondent's subsequent motion to recall and revoke that order was denied, and her purported appeal from that non-appealable order (Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 6126.3(l)) was dismissed by the Court of Appeal. (No. A160546 (Feb. 23, 2021.) In her latest motion, Respondent continues to insist that she is authorized to represent aliens in federal immigration proceedings. However, Respondent's argument is inconsistent with federal regulations (8 C.F.R. Sec. 292.1(a)), and ignores undisputed evidence presented to the Court by the State Bar in support of its original verified application which establishes that Respondent is not registered with the Secretary of State as an immigration consultant; that the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services has formally notified her of her ineligibility to practice before USCIS and has sent her a cease and desist order; that she is "not authorized to practice immigration law in the United States"; is not recognized as an accredited representative, and that her businesses are not recognized organizations under the Executive Office for Immigration Review. (Alcon Decl. pars. 8, 34-35 & Exs. 3, 26; Decl. of Catherine M. O'Connell [Disciplinary Counsel for USCIS] pars. 7, 8 & Exs. A, B.) It is long past time for Respondent to wake up to reality: she may not lawfully practice law in California, and that if she attempts to do so, she may be subject to criminal prosecution. (Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 6126(a).)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion 1. To Recall And Revoke 4/29/20 Order; 2. To Dissolve The Injunction
Off Calendar Aug-03-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (through Zoom):
Abrahim Bagheri, Deputy Trial Counsel, 213-765-1216, abrahim.bagheri@calbar.ca.gov of the Office of Chief Trial Counsel() for Petitioner.
Nelly Allen, Esq. 415-378-0669, Respondent In Pro Per.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. Respondent argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

RESPONDENT NELLY ALLEN's Motion 1. To Recall And Revoke 4/29/20 Order; 2. To Dissolve The Injunction.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Respondent Nelly Allen Gabueva's motion to recall and revoke the court's April 29, 2020 order is denied. On June 29, 2020, Respondent filed a notice of appeal from that order. However, the order "is not appealable and shall not be stayed by petition for a writ, except as ordered by the superior court or by the appellate court." (Bus. & Prof. Code 6126.3(l).) The court declines to revisit or stay its order, for at least two reasons.

First, Respondent's motion amounts to a motion for reconsideration, which must be denied as untimely. (See Code Civ. Proc. 1008(a) [motion for reconsideration must be filed within 10 days after service of notice of entry of order].) Further, to the extent that the motion purports to be based on new or different facts, Respondent fails to "provide a satisfactory explanation for failing to present the evidence sooner." (California Correctional Peace Officers Ass'n v. Virga (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 30, 47, fn. 15; see also Garcia v. Hejmadi (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 674, 690 ["strict requirement of diligence"].)

Second, Respondent's motion lacks any colorable factual or legal basis. As Respondent admits, she is "not admitted to the State Bar" of California. (Gabueva Decl., par. 5.) The court's April 29, 2020 order granting the Bar's application for assumption of jurisdiction over Respondent's unauthorized law practice was based on substantial-indeed, overwhelming-evidence submitted by the State Bar that Respondent nevertheless was "advertising or holding herself out as practicing or entitled to practice law in California, and [was] otherwise practicing law without authority." (Order, 3.) Respondent's "affidavit" in support of her motion, which consists largely of incoherent legal argument, does not rebut or even address any of that evidence, and in any event is unsworn and therefore inadmissible. (See Code Civ. Proc. 2015.5.) Entry of this court's order was warranted in order to avoid prejudice to the interests of clients or other interested persons or entities affected by Respondent's unauthorized practice of law. (See Bus. & Prof. Code 6125, 6126(a), 6126.3, and 6030.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
